steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Recall the standard form of a circle equation
steps.content: A circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ always follows the standard form below. Notice that the center coordinates appear inside the parentheses (with signs flipped), and the radius squared is the constant on the right.

steps.title: Read off h from the center
steps.content: We're given that the center is at $(5, 1)$. In the standard form, the center is $(h, k)$, so the $x$-coordinate of the center is $h = 5$. That's our answer.
